_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
- **1 February 2023** — H2Accelerate TRUCKS project start date; Grant Agreement 101101446 signed under Clean Hydrogen Partnership (Horizon Europe Joint Undertaking). *In force as of 1 February 2023.* *Source: Clean Hydrogen Partnership Project Dashboard. https://www.clean-hydrogen.europa.eu/projects-dashboard/projects-repository/h2accelerate-trucks_en.*

- **14 March 2023** — Clean Hydrogen Partnership publicly announces €30 million grant for H2Accelerate TRUCKS (150 fuel cell trucks; original consortium: Daimler Truck, Volvo Group, Iveco Group) and parallel CEF funding for eight heavy-duty hydrogen refuelling stations in France and the Netherlands. *In force as of 14 March 2023.* *Source: Clean Hydrogen Partnership News Release. https://www.clean-hydrogen.europa.eu/media/news/h2accelerate-deploy-150-fuel-cell-trucks-across-eu-2023-03-14_en.*

- **13 September 2023** — AFIR (Regulation (EU) 2023/1804) adopted; sets binding hydrogen refuelling station deployment targets for EU member states along TEN-T core network. *Source: EUR-Lex CELEX:32023R1804. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32023R1804.*

- **13 April 2024** — AFIR becomes directly applicable in all EU member states; member state obligations on hydrogen refuelling infrastructure deployment begin. *In force as of 13 April 2024.* *Source: EC DG MOVE, Alternative Fuels Infrastructure page. https://transport.ec.europa.eu/transport-themes/clean-transport/alternative-fuels-infrastructure_en.*

- **14 May 2024** — Regulation (EU) 2024/1610 published, amending HDV CO2 standards (Regulation (EU) 2019/1242); tightens fleet-average emission reduction targets for heavy-duty vehicles from 2030. *In force as of May 2024.* *Source: EUR-Lex OJ:L_202401610. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=OJ:L_202401610.*

- **November 2025** — EU awards over €600 million under AFIR aegis to 70 projects to decarbonise TEN-T transport infrastructure, including installation of 38 hydrogen refuelling stations for cars, trucks, and buses. *Source: European Hydrogen Observatory, AFIR entry. https://observatory.clean-hydrogen.europa.eu/eu-policy/alternative-fuels-infrastructure-regulation.*

- **November 2025** — Political agreement reached on EU transport GHG accounting regulation (COM(2023) 441); implementing rules pending. *Source: EC DG MOVE announcement. https://transport.ec.europa.eu/news-events/news/commission-welcomes-political-agreement-new-rules-harmonise-transport-emissions-calculations-eu-2025-11-06_en.*

- **January 2026** — H2Accelerate TRUCKS consortium restructured: Iveco Group and Shell conclude participation; Scania, Hyundai Hydrogen Mobility Germany, and Hyliko join; revised target is 125 fuel cell trucks across ≥6 EU member states. First vehicles expected to enter operation during 2026. *Source: H2Accelerate,
